case.question: The square piece of paper is folded in half diagonally, and two different shapes are punched through the folded layers. Which of the labeled options shows the correct pattern of holes when the paper is completely unfolded?
case.rubric: Folding diagonally from bottom-left to top-right acts as a reflection across that diagonal; the square hole punched near the top-left corner reflects across to the bottom-right corner, while the circular hole punched near the top-right corner reflects to an adjacent spot still in the top-right quadrant, making Option C the only mathematically correct unfolding.

planner_focus.weakness: reasoning about folding, unfolding, hidden structure, orientation, or spatial adjacency

case.prompt: Create one BabyVision-style visual logical puzzle image. Use a clean printed worksheet style on a white background, crisp geometric shapes, small labels, no photorealism, no watermark, and no explanatory answer key. It must be unambiguous and solvable from the image alone. Do not include the written question in the image. A clean, printed worksheet puzzle on a white background testing spatial reasoning. Crisp black lines, no photorealism, no watermark. The top half shows a two-step paper folding diagram. Panel 1 shows a square with a dashed diagonal fold-line extending from the bottom-left corner to the top-right corner. A curved arrow indicates the bottom-right half of the square folding up and over to meet the top-left corner. Panel 2 shows the resulting folded shape: a right-angled triangle with its 90-degree corner at the top-left. On this triangle, two holes are drawn as if punched through: a small square-shaped hole located near the top-left corner (the 90-degree corner), and a small circular hole located near the far top-right corner (the sharp upper point). The bottom half of the image shows four large unfolded squares, labeled A, B, C, and D, each displaying a different pattern of holes. Option A shows square holes at the top-left and top-right, and circular holes at the bottom-left and bottom-right. Option B shows square holes at the top-left and bottom-left, and circular holes at the top-right and bottom-right. Option C shows one square hole near the top-left corner and a second square hole near the bottom-right corner, along with two circular holes grouped closely together near the top-right corner. Option D shows square holes at the top-left and bottom-right, and two circular holes grouped near the bottom-left corner. Make Option C exactly as described.
